case_name: Remote surface-water supply with pesticide removal under pilot-only and capital limits
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible drinking-water treatment process chains for pesticide-impacted surface water that best balances removal performance, pilotability, capital limits, remote operation, and residuals management.

input_data.scenario_background: A remote mountain town relies on a small river intake that receives seasonal agricultural runoff from upstream orchards and mixed-use land. Recent pilot monitoring found propoxur and tebufenozide in the raw water together with moderate natural organic matter and periodic turbidity spikes during storms. The utility has funding only for a modest pilot-to-deployment pathway, limited access to specialist technicians, and high hauling costs for difficult residuals, so the preferred solution must balance pesticide control with simple operation and realistic long-term support.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should reliably achieve potable-water quality with strong reduction of detected pesticides and co-contaminants while maintaining stable compliance during seasonal source-water variability.
secondary_goals: limit specialized operator attention in a remote location
avoid residual streams that are difficult to transport or dispose
use treatment steps that can be piloted at small scale before full commitment
retain robustness during episodic turbidity and organic matter increases

engineering_constraints.residuals_management: prefer residuals compatible with standard backwash sludge handling or periodic media changeout; avoid continuous hazardous concentrate generation
engineering_constraints.robustness_requirement: treatment must tolerate seasonal runoff events, moderate NOM competition, and be practical for phased pilot-to-full-scale implementation in a remote community

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: reduce turbidity, algae, and a portion of natural organic matter to protect downstream adsorption
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: remove remaining particulates and stabilize filtered water quality
core_unit_functions.powdered_activated_carbon_seasonal_dosin: provide flexible adsorption of episodic pesticides, taste and odor compounds, and cyanotoxin co-contaminants during peaks
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_biologically_a: polish residual pesticides and dissolved organics while buffering breakthrough risk
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbial protection for potable supply

applicability_rationale: surface water with moderate DOC and storm-driven turbidity benefits from conventional clarification ahead of adsorption so carbon capacity is not wasted on particulates and excess organic matter
a PAC plus GAC approach balances seasonal flexibility and more stable polishing for propoxur and tebufenozide, both of which are generally amenable to activated carbon adsorption
this train can be piloted stepwise using portable PAC feed and lead-lag GAC vessels before larger capital commitment
the route fits remote operation better than high-pressure membrane systems because it avoids concentrate handling and very high energy dependence
co-contaminants such as geosmin and low-level microcystin increase the value of activated carbon beyond just the two primary pesticides
potential_risks: moderate NOM can shorten activated carbon life and requires monitoring of bed breakthrough
PAC handling and dust control still require operator training
performance during extreme runoff events depends on maintaining effective coagulation and filter operation

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: remove suspended solids and reduce organic loading
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: produce low-turbidity water for adsorption treatment
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_lead_lag: adsorb pesticides and provide operational redundancy by using a staged carbon arrangement
core_unit_functions.disinfection: ensure microbiological safety in finished water

applicability_rationale: lead-lag GAC is a strong and relatively simple route for hydrophobic to moderately adsorbable pesticides in clarified surface water
it is easier to operate remotely than membrane or ozone-based advanced processes
the staged bed arrangement improves compliance confidence and allows practical media change scheduling
compared with adding PAC, this route reduces daily chemical handling and may suit a remote utility with fewer operators
potential_risks: without PAC, short-term contaminant spikes may push more load onto GAC and reduce run length
higher DOC periods can accelerate breakthrough and require frequent monitoring
less flexible than PAC-assisted operation when seasonal runoff is highly episodic

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: remove particulates and reduce ozone demand from raw surface water
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: provide low-turbidity water to improve downstream oxidation performance
core_unit_functions.ozonation: oxidize susceptible organic micropollutants and help with taste and odor control
core_unit_functions.biologically_active_filtration: remove biodegradable oxidation byproducts and polish residual organics

applicability_rationale: ozone followed by biofiltration is technically plausible for broad micropollutant management and can also assist with geosmin control
this route may be attractive where the utility wants multi-benefit treatment for organics and aesthetic episodes beyond only pesticide adsorption
pre-clarification is appropriate because raw surface water turbidity and DOC would otherwise raise oxidant demand and reduce process stability
potential_risks: oxidation effectiveness for specific pesticides can be compound-dependent and may be less straightforward to validate than adsorption
remote operation is challenged by ozone generation equipment, process control, and maintenance demands
bromate formation risk must be assessed if bromide is present even at low levels

core_unit_functions.enhanced_coagulation: lower fouling load and organic matter prior to membranes
core_unit_functions.ultrafiltration: remove fine particles and provide reliable pretreatment protection
core_unit_functions.nanofiltration: reject a broad range of organic micropollutants including pesticides
core_unit_functions.stabilization: adjust treated water chemistry after membrane treatment

applicability_rationale: nanofiltration is technically capable of strong pesticide reduction and can provide a robust barrier for multiple co-contaminants
this option may be favored where very stringent future contaminant uncertainty drives preference for physical separation
it offers compact process units that can be skid-mounted for pilot work
potential_risks: concentrate management is difficult and expensive in a remote location
capital and power demand are misaligned with the town's budget and energy sensitivity
membrane fouling control and specialist maintenance needs exceed the preferred operating complexity

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: remove turbidity and reduce UV-absorbing load ahead of oxidation
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: protect UV system by producing low-turbidity water
core_unit_functions.advanced_oxidation_uv_hydrogen_peroxide: generate hydroxyl radicals for oxidation of susceptible organic contaminants

applicability_rationale: UV-based advanced oxidation is included as a technically possible but generally less aligned option for this pesticide-dominated remote surface-water case
it can be considered if pilot tests show strong transformation of the target compounds and if the utility also values a high-intensity oxidation barrier
potential_risks: moderate UV254 and DOC increase oxidant demand and reduce treatment economy
compound-specific performance may be uncertain compared with adsorption and requires careful pilot validation
chemical supply logistics for hydrogen peroxide and UV equipment maintenance are unfavorable in a remote setting
oxidation byproducts or incomplete transformation products may require additional evaluation
